CHASE
Heads it is! Let's try again.
JOE
Heads'
20-
Peter shows him the coin heads up -- Joe shrieks with delight.
DAVID
Joe -- sing your song'
Joe sings a song about monkeys that the kids have heard a
hundred times.
The boys clap,
Lux hugs Joe.
JOE
(singing)
Oh the monkeys have no tails
in Samba Wango! Oh the Monkeys
have no tails, they were bitten
off by whales, oh the monkeys
have no tails in Samba Wango'
and the Lisbon girls clap and sing along, and
They are all starting to have a good time.
Cecilia slips off her stool.

CUT TO:
39*
40*
EXT. THE NEIGHBORHOOD - DAY
DOLLY DOWN THE STREET
-24-
It's a sunny day, people mow their lawns, someone barbecues.
NARRATOR
We didn't understand why Cecilia
had killed herself the first
time and we understood even
less when she did it twice.
INT. TIM WEINER'S BEDROOM - DAY
CUT TO:
C.U.- The Lisbon girls' possessions the boys have started to
collect are lined up on the floor around them: A Bonnie Lisbon
tardy pass, a bottle of nail polish, a photo of Mary (she's
been cut out from the background of a photo from their
street), the party invitation, a scarf that had been left
outside, Cecilia's diary.
The group of neighborhood boys crowd around a book.
CLOSE ON DIARY
The cover is drawn on with marker to look like a medieval
bible.

CUT TO:
Past corny flower arrangement after flower arrangement, all
with cards: "With Sympathy" or "Our Condolences," etc.
Camera ends up on Mr. Lisbon's Lay-Z-boy chair.
is staring at the TV, lost in a baseball game.
RINGS, Mr. Lisbon doesn't notice.
Mary answers the door to see Father Moody.
FATHER MOODY
Hi Mary, are your parents home?
MARY
Oh, hi, Father Moody. Come
in.
Mary leads him over to her father.
MR. LISBON
Hello Father, please sit down.
Mr. Lisbon
The doorbell
He continues watching the game, as if everything's normal.
MR. LISBON
(talks to TV baseball
players)
C'mon Miller! ... that was
inside'
47A
He drinks a beer, and offers one to Father Moody.

INT. TRIP'S LIVING ROOM (PRESENT DAY - '75)
-40-
CUT TO:
Trip sits on a bar stool at a bar nook in the dark wood room
with hanging plants. He leans on the oounter in confused­
deep concentration, love sick. His DAD -- in a silk kimono -­
pats him on the back, as his Dad's friend DONALD (with side­
swept hair and a Burt Reynolds mustache) hands him a drink.
DONALD
Well first off don't call her
on the phone -- it's all
subtlety, it's all nuance.
MR. FONTAINE
You just need to talk to her
at school -- about anything -­
the weather, school assignments,
anything to have the chance to
communicate with the language
of eye contact.

asks him a question.
INTERVIEWER (V.O.)
Do you think it's possible
that Lux"s passion toward you
could have come from a misplaced
need~
ADULT TRIP
(offended)
What are you saying, that I
was just a vehicle? You can't
fake that, man. It was real.
CUT TO:
-46-
INT. MRS. LISBON'S CONDO - DAY
FLASH FORWARD: Mrs. Lisbon, weathered,sits
her modest condo, next to a plastic plant.
her neck is visible over a drab blouse.

The ballot Jar is over at the
refreshments table, so please
cast your votes and we'll make
the announcement in a little
while.
116A INT. SCHOOL - GYM - BLEACHERS - NIGHT
As he talks, Trip and Lux slip under the bleachers. Parkie
follows Bonnie after them, and in the slits of light coming
through the bleachers he sees Trip and Lux sitting closely,
as Trip holds a bottle up to Lux's face.
LUX
Did anybody see you come in?
BONNIE
No.
116A CONTINUED:
LUX
What about you?
PARKIE
No.
-71-
They are silent, and their attention returns to the label of
the bottle in Trip's hand.

On the steps Lux slides a crate down - she pushes to send it
crashing -- but then grabs it just before it goes out of
control.
Lux puts one record at a time into the flames as she cries.
She holds some up, pleading to her mother.
LUX
"Destroyer" not Destroyer 1
Mrs. Lisbon does not respond, but points to the fire. Lux
dramatically sacrifices it to the flames.
LUX
Aw, not AC/DC' 1 ' 1 -- c'mon' ...
But Lux has to keep putting her record collection, one by
one, into the fire. Lux starts coughing, thick grey smoke
is coming out of the fireplace, getting worse and worse from
the burning plastic. Mrs. Lisbon covers her mouth with her
hand.
THERESE
(calls from upstairs)
What's that smell? Is like
the linoleum on fire?
Mrs. Lisbon coughs. The room is filling with thick smoke.
Lux is about to add another record to the fire.

FADE IN:
CHASE
I gotta go home, see you
tomorrow ...
INT. TIM WEINER'S BEDROOM - DAY
The boys are calling again, but there is no answer.
TIM
Wait, look --
-97-
FADE OUT:
They look across the street and see the girls doing something
in their room. Chase looks through the telescope.
161A EXT. (#2) LISBON HOUSE - GIRLS' BEDROOM - DAY
TELESCOPE POV:
The girls are packing a big trunk, filling it with clothes
and books. In a huff Bonnie takes out some clothes to make
room for Mary
s stuff. Mary puts in her make-up mirror, a
radio, a hair dryer ...
